question.label: Alignment friction
question.name: What part of the alignment work is taking more effort than it should?
question.description: Finds the practical friction in running alignment without making it abstract.

answer: I want to drive alignment, but I do not want to create noise by over-communicating.

assessment.note: She names a specific blocker in alignment work: uncertainty about the right communication level, which signals low clarity with mild drag on confidence and growth.

new_queue.label: Over-communicating risk
new_queue.name: What makes something feel like noise in those cross-team updates?

new_queue.label: Manager boundaries
new_queue.name: What boundaries from me would help most right now?

issues: coverage: injected growth into next question (0 touches after turn 5)

history.answer_excerpt: I am settling in, but I still feel like I am learning the unwritten rules in every meeting.
I can draft plans, though I am not always sure when I should decide versus escalate.

I want to drive alignment, but I do not want to create noise by over-communicating.
Cross-team conversations move fast and I sometimes leave unsure about who committed to what.
Being remote makes it harder to read context that others seem to pick up naturally.
